wedge.chosen: AgentReady harness — a live, reproducible 'agent task-success rate' metric that attributes each agent failure to a specific doc gap, and shows a before/after jump when the gap is patched.
runner_ups: Agent-driven code-snippet verifier that runs every doc code sample against a live API and flags failures
GEO surface-area scorer that rates how discoverable/citable docs are to AI models
wedge.rationale: Turns Manicule's marketing slogan ('agents perform no errors with our approach vs failing 25% of the time') into a live, per-gap-attributed metric — exactly the AgentRel thesis, and shows off agentic-AI harness engineering.

spec.magic_moment: On one screen, a real Claude tool-use agent is turned loose on a bundled synthetic API's docs and attempts a suite of concrete developer tasks. On the FIRST run against the deliberately-gapped docs, the AgentReady harness reports a task-success rate of ~70-75% and traces each of the ~25% failures to a specific missing or contradictory doc section. You click 'Apply doc patch,' which fills exactly those gaps, and re-run: the same agent now completes the task suite at ~100%. The before/after success-rate number climbs live, and each fixed failure flips from red to green.
in_scope: A single bundled synthetic REST API (MemStore) served by a local mock server with a real OpenAPI spec
Two docs fixtures: docs_gapped/ (deliberately missing/contradictory) and docs_fixed/ (complete)
A task suite of ~8-12 concrete natural-language developer tasks
A real Claude tool-use agent loop that reads ONLY provided docs, calls the mock API via a tool, self-reports; a deterministic checker validates real API side effects
A web dashboard: task grid, success-rate meter, per-failure gap traces, Apply-doc-patch / Re-run controls
out_of_scope: Crawling real third-party or production APIs
Auto-writing the doc fix with an LLM (fix is a pre-authored fixture swap)
stack: Python 3.11 + FastAPI (mock MemStore API + harness backend, uvicorn)
Anthropic Python SDK for the tool-use agent loop; API key via .env
SQLite / in-memory dict for the mock store
Vanilla JS + fetch dashboard served by FastAPI
JSON fixtures for docs, task suite, cached replay

smoke_condition: The full pipeline runs end-to-end: the Claude tool-use agent, reading only docs_gapped/, executes the task suite against the local mock API and scores strictly below 100% (at least 2 tasks fail), each failure is attributed to a named doc gap, and after swapping to docs_fixed/ the same agent re-runs and scores ~100% with all previously-failing tasks turning green — the before/after success-rate jump and per-gap traces are visible on the dashboard, and the pytest smoke test asserting gapped < 100% and fixed == 100% exits 0.

profile.product: Manicule (YC P26, founded 2025 by Naman Bansal and Shreyans Jain) is a technical documentation studio / DevRel-as-a-service for developer-tool startups, pivoting toward 'AgentRel' — optimizing docs for discovery and correct use by AI agents. 7-step pipeline: Audit -> Information Architecture -> Writing+Review -> Code Verification -> Videos+Screenshots -> Final Push -> Creative Direction.
profile.key_claim: agents perform no errors while using our approach vs failing 25% of the time with other frameworks
profile.positioning: Humans Do the Thinking, Agents Do the Scale Work; twice as fast with AI and half the cost of hiring a DevRel; ship in <2 weeks
profile.supermemory_case: SQL-based metadata filtering was an undocumented feature discovered from the codebase; +30% answer success rate; 23-day turnaround; killed 70% of confusion with a 'zero to aha' journey
profile.tech_signals: Free 7-minute Audit tool; agents draft from OpenAPI specs + SDK definitions; docs on Mintlify; Content Engineer role owns 'prompts, evals, the harness around them'; 'Agent error rate: 0% vs 25% with competing frameworks'
